title: Implement TCP listener and select event loop
description: Implement the server executable entry point, socket setup, bind, listen, accept, and single-process select loop. Keep this task focused on accepting clients and detecting readable file descriptors.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN ./server 8888 starts THEN it listens on the requested port; WHEN a client connects THEN accept succeeds and the client fd is tracked; WHEN multiple client fds exist THEN select handles readiness without per-client threads; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:96
review_initial_requirements: Review error paths for socket, bind, listen, accept, and select before adding higher-level chat behavior.
review_regression_requirements: Verify implementation remains single-process select-based and does not switch to threads, epoll, database, or GUI behavior.

title: Implement client state management
description: Implement ClientManager for fd, nickname, login state, lookup by fd, lookup by nickname, duplicate nickname detection, removal, and online user list generation.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN a new fd is accepted THEN a client record can be added; WHEN login succeeds THEN nickname and login state are stored; WHEN a client disconnects THEN the fd and nickname are removed; WHEN LIST is requested THEN current online users are returned; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:112
review_initial_requirements: Review data ownership so closing a socket cannot leave stale nickname state.
review_regression_requirements: Verify duplicate nickname rejection and online-list generation match the approved ClientManager responsibilities.

title: Implement login and lifecycle handling
description: Implement server-side handling for LOGIN, QUIT, normal disconnect, and abnormal disconnect. Broadcast system messages and write logs for user lifecycle events.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN a valid unique nickname logs in THEN OK is returned and other users receive an online SYSTEM notice; WHEN nickname is empty, invalid, or duplicate THEN the documented ERROR is returned; WHEN QUIT or recv 0 occurs THEN state is cleaned, fd is closed, log is written, and other users receive offline notice; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:194
review_initial_requirements: Review validation order and cleanup order before implementing message routing.
review_regression_requirements: Verify lifecycle behavior covers normal exit, abnormal terminal close, duplicate nickname, invalid nickname, and not-logged-in command handling.

title: Implement chat message routing
description: Implement MessageRouter behavior for MSG broadcast, PRIVATE delivery, LIST response, and system notifications. Preserve the approved rule that group messages go to other online users and private messages only go to the target.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN user A sends MSG THEN other online users receive CHAT A content; WHEN user A sends PRIVATE B content THEN only B receives PRIVATE A content; WHEN target is absent THEN A receives ERROR user not online; WHEN LIST is requested THEN only requester receives USERLIST; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:137
review_initial_requirements: Review send failure behavior so one bad client does not break delivery to other clients.
review_regression_requirements: Verify routing does not echo group chat to the sender unless deliberately documented, and that private messages never leak to other clients.

title: Harden server error handling and resource cleanup
description: Complete server-side error handling for invalid commands, oversized messages, recv/send failures, and resource cleanup. Keep behavior aligned with the approved error response strings.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN an invalid command arrives THEN ERROR invalid command is returned; WHEN an oversized message arrives THEN ERROR message too long is returned and the server remains running; WHEN send or recv fails THEN the affected fd is cleaned without stopping unrelated clients; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:221
review_initial_requirements: Review all close paths for double-close and stale fd_set state.
review_regression_requirements: Verify server remains usable after malformed input, long input, abrupt client close, and send or recv errors.

title: Review server module against approved behavior
description: Use a same-model sub-agent review to compare the delivered server behavior, select concurrency model, routing, lifecycle cleanup, logs, and validation evidence against the approved design.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN all non-review issues before this row are closed THEN run same-model sub-agent review against source-specific claim/evidence checks; WHEN gaps or overstated claims are found THEN append follow-up issues and REVIEW-02; WHEN no gaps remain THEN close the CSV.
review_initial_requirements: Verify all prior non-review rows are closed before running this review.
review_regression_requirements: Check that the server is a Linux TCP socket server using select rather than per-client threads; Check that login, duplicate nickname rejection, group chat, private chat, LIST, QUIT, abnormal disconnect, and long-message errors have evidence; Check that server.log records the approved event types; Check that non-goals such as database, GUI, offline history, and admin features are absent.
